nodes.label: CalloraVoipSdk SDK
nodes.rationale: Commercial-grade .NET VoIP SDK (net8.0/net9.0/net10.0) for softphones, PBX integrations, contact-center workflows and voice automation; built for developers who need more than a black-box telephony wrapper. Apache-2.0 licensed core.

nodes.label: VoipClient Public Facade
nodes.rationale: VoipClient is the central, supported integration surface; Infrastructure types are internal implementation details and Application types are only exposed where necessary — keeps the external API compact and stable while allowing internal evolution.

nodes.label: CalloraVoipSdk.Audio.Windows Package
nodes.rationale: Windows audio integration based on NAudio.

nodes.label: CalloraVoipSdk.Audio.Linux Package
nodes.rationale: Linux audio integration based on PortAudio.

nodes.label: Module Registry (client.Modules)
nodes.rationale: Extension point for feature modules shipped as separate packages: implement IVoipClientModule, attach, resolve by any implemented interface via Get<T>/TryGet<T>.

nodes.label: Per-Call Media Tap
nodes.rationale: IMediaReceiver.FrameReceived fires synchronously on the media path — handlers must buffer and return immediately, never block. Negotiated format available via ICall.MediaParameters.

nodes.label: DDD-Oriented Architecture
nodes.rationale: Domain (entities, value objects, states, domain events), Application (use cases, orchestration), Infrastructure (SIP/RTP/SDP/audio implementations), Client (facade, convenience APIs, DI) — clear boundaries so infrastructure stays a non-public integration surface.

nodes.label: Commercial Plugin Line-up (Callora.Realtime/WebSocket/Privacy/Risk/Intelligence)
nodes.rationale: SDK core stays open and free; advanced capabilities ship as paid plugins on a private feed (not nuget.org), licensed separately — e.g. Callora.Realtime bridges call audio to realtime AI APIs with pacing, backpressure and barge-in.

edges.source_location: On top of this extension point
Modules build on the public per-call media tap
